blocks.text: Student application call
blocks.textWithoutLinks: Student application call

blocks.text: The application period for a scholarship to join our Master Program in NeuroData will open soon! All applications should be submitted through our online system and meet the deadline. Paper applications or applications sent by electronic mail will not be considered.
blocks.textWithoutLinks: The application period for a scholarship to join our Master Program in NeuroData will open soon! All applications should be submitted through our online system and meet the deadline. Paper applications or applications sent by electronic mail will not be considered.

blocks.text: The application system will be open from November 15th 2025, to February 5th 2026 (SUBMISSION DEADLINE). Any application submitted after February 05th, 2026, will be automatically rejected.
blocks.textWithoutLinks: The application system will be open from November 15th 2025, to February 5th 2026 (SUBMISSION DEADLINE). Any application submitted after February 05th, 2026, will be automatically rejected.

blocks.text: We remind you that the English test (IELTS – TOEFL – CAE – CPE) is mandatory to join our programme. Upon the selection committee's review and evaluation of their applications, selected students will be contacted for an interview in early March. The final results of the selection will be published in April 2026.
blocks.textWithoutLinks: We remind you that the English test (IELTS – TOEFL – CAE – CPE) is mandatory to join our programme. Upon the selection committee's review and evaluation of their applications, selected students will be contacted for an interview in early March. The final results of the selection will be published in April 2026.

blocks.text: For any questions, please contact our Helpdesk: neuro.data@biu.ac.il
blocks.textWithoutLinks: For any questions, please contact our Helpdesk:

blocks.text: neuro.data@biu.ac.il
Personal Data Use
blocks.textWithoutLinks: Personal Data Use

blocks.text: The personal data of selected candidates/applicants (proposed for a scholarship/fellowship, put on the reserve list or enrolled on a self-paying basis) may be used to evaluate the programmes, efficiently manage the projects, and produce statistics. Furthermore, data could be made available to the EACEA, the European Commission, the European External Action Service staff, and other stakeholders of the Erasmus+ programme, such as Erasmus+ National Agencies, National Erasmus+ Offices and the Erasmus Mundus Student and Alumni Association.
blocks.textWithoutLinks: The personal data of selected candidates/applicants (proposed for a scholarship/fellowship, put on the reserve list or enrolled on a self-paying basis) may be used to evaluate the programmes, efficiently manage the projects, and produce statistics. Furthermore, data could be made available to the EACEA, the European Commission, the European External Action Service staff, and other stakeholders of the Erasmus+ programme, such as Erasmus+ National Agencies, National Erasmus+ Offices and the Erasmus Mundus Student and Alumni Association.

blocks.text: The European Education and Culture Executive Agency (EACEA), in the context of managing the Erasmus Mundus Joint Masters, collects and processes the personal data of some of the candidates. In particular, certain data of the scholarship holders and non-scholarship holders is shared with the Agency and treated according to this privacy statement.
blocks.textWithoutLinks: The European Education and Culture Executive Agency (EACEA), in the context of managing the Erasmus Mundus Joint Masters, collects and processes the personal data of some of the candidates. In particular, certain data of the scholarship holders and non-scholarship holders is shared with the Agency and treated according to this privacy statement.

blocks.text: Application Instructions
blocks.textWithoutLinks: Application Instructions

blocks.text: All candidates (applying for a scholarship or under a self-funded scheme) must fill in the online application form and submit it by the given deadlines with the following attachments. Students with double nationality must specify the nationality under which they submit their application. Students may indicate up to three study tracks in order of preference for their second-year specialization and mobility. During the application process, applicants will be asked to rank three institutions where they want to perform the second year of the master's. The selection committee will consider this ranking but does not promise that all applicants will be granted their first mobility selection. Selected applicants will be informed of their assigned mobility pathway. Students can ask to change their second-year mobility during the first year. The Administrative Board will consider this appeal, and its decision will be final. The application will not be processed if any flowing documents are missing.

The selection committee will consider this ranking but does not promise that all applicants will be granted their first mobility selection. Selected applicants will be informed of their assigned mobility pathway. Students can ask to change their second-year mobility during the first year. The Administrative Board will consider this appeal, and its decision will be final. The application will not be processed if any flowing documents are missing.

blocks.text: A calculation of the cumulative GPA normalized to a scale of 0-100. For example, if the maximal GPA in your country is 4 and your cumulative GPA is 3.8, then the normalized cumulative GPA is 100*3.8/4=95.
